title: [packit] Propose downstream failed for release v1.21.0
body:
Packit failed on creating pull-requests in dist-git (https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/skopeo.git):
dist-git brancherror
f41See https://dashboard.packit.dev/jobs/propose-downstream/22779
f42See https://dashboard.packit.dev/jobs/propose-downstream/22778
f43See https://dashboard.packit.dev/jobs/propose-downstream/22777
rawhideSee https://dashboard.packit.dev/jobs/propose-downstream/22780
You can retrigger the update by adding a comment (`/packit propose-downstream`) into this issue.

comments.body: @lsm5 is this expected? At a glance, something seems to be breaking about the `Version: 0` in the spec file. Guessing, does the tagged version need to be an ancestor commit of `main`?

> [@lsm5](https://github.com/lsm5) is this expected? At a glance, something seems to be breaking about the `Version: 0` in the spec file. Guessing, does the tagged version need to be an ancestor commit of `main`?

very likely the `get-current-version` action in `.packit.yaml` failed. I'll check on that and fix for the next release. I'll bump this one manually.
